ddt:Target.Name: UK33_C1A_UDS_v1.8_210701
ddt:Target.Description:
Control for UK33 CAN C1A Vehicle : HHN-HHC Software version : 009A-000x
2020-02-05
Dongkwon KIM (dongkwon.kim@renault.com)
2020-07-01
Add Soft Version 009A-0005. Modify DTC and DTC's fault types
2020-08-31
Modify DTC and DTC's fault types. Add DIDs and modify DIDs.
2020-09-02
Modify DID error.
2020-09-17
Add DID $F0D0 and $F0D1. Modify Auto-identification (add SW 9A-06, 9A-07).
2020-10-06
Modify Communication protocol to DiagOnCANFD
2021-03-09
Modify Auto-identification(add SW9A-08). Add DTCs.
2021-06-28
Modify Request, Data and DIDs. Add Soft Version 009A-0009 and 009A-0010. 2021-12-6 Identification added by tool (CORP\k191233) => DiagVersion=54, Supplier=APY, Soft=009A, Version=0011
2022-3-21 Identification added by tool (CORP\k191233) => DiagVersion=54, Supplier=APY, Soft=009A, Version=0012
2022-7-14 Identification added by tool (CORP\k191233) => DiagVersion=54, Supplier=APY, Soft=009A, Version=00A0

Item.Text: Lost Communication with VDC(C1A-HS FD CAN)
Lost Communication with ECM(PT CAN) for network
Lost Communication with ECM(PT CAN) for OBD
Select pressure solenoid Circuit failure(short to power)
Select pressure solenoid Circuit failure (short to ground)
Secondary pully speed sensor No signal
Secondary pully speed sensor Output voltage failure
Electric Oil Pump Main unit failure
Primary Pressure Solenoid Circuit failure (short to power)
Primary Pressure Solenoid Circuit failure (short to ground)
Line Pressure solenoid Circuit failure (short to power)
Line Pressure solenoid Circuit failure (short to ground)
Primary pully speed sensor No signal
Primary pully speed sensor Rationality Other
Primary pully speed sensor Output voltage failure
Secondary Pressure Solenoid Circuit failure (short to ground)
Turbine speed sensor Output voltage failure
Lost Communication with VDC(C1A-HS PT CAN)
Inhibitor switch Circuit failure of switch 1
Inhibitor switch Circuit failure of switch 2
Inhibitor switch Circuit failure of switch 3
Inhibitor switch Circuit failure of switch 4
Primary pully speed sensor Circuit low(short to ground)
Primary pully speed sensor Circuit high(open or short to power)
Primary pully speed sensor Out of Range
Secondary pully speed sensor Circuit low(short to ground)
Secondary pully speed sensor Circuit high(open or short to power)
Secondary pully speed sensor Rationality Other
Secondary pully speed sensor Out of Range
Turbine speed sensor Circuit low(short to ground)
Turbine speed sensor Circuit high(open or short to power)
Turbine speed sensor Out of Range
Lock-up Solenoid Circuit failure (short to ground)
Lock-up Solenoid Circuit failure (short to power)
Secondary Pressure Solenoid Circuit failure (short to power)
Electric Oil Pump Functional failure (flapper valve stuck closed)
Electric Oil Pump Functional failure (check valve stuck closed)
Electric Oil Pump Functional failure (check valve stuck open)
Electric Oil Pump Functional failure (flapper valve stuck open)
Electric Oil Pump Functional failure (belt slip)
Sudden deceleration due to unintended sudden downshift
Unintentional sudden acceleration during step downshift
Lost Communication with ECM(C1A-HS FD CAN)
Lost Communication with SCU(5th generation CAN)
Device and failure type ODB codding
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed or blocked
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Item.Text: OccurrenceCounter (Counts the number of times the DTC have been set.)

Data.Name: Paddle down switch Paddle up switch.Paddle Down status
Paddle down switch Paddle up switch.Paddle Up switch
ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: CVTBelt stick active test progress rate
Hi speed charge leaning step value_Hi temp
Hi speed charge leaning step value_normal temp
Contact point learning step value_low temp
Contact point learning step value_normal temp
Hi speed charge leaning step value_low temp R side
Hi speed charge leaning step value_normal temp R side
DataRead $130D.CVT Belt stick active test Done
DataRead $130D.CVT Belt stick active test result
DataRead $130E.DRV hydraulic vibration judder NG flag
DataRead $130F.LU judder serge line pressure UP flag
DataRead $1310.hydraulic vibration detect flag_shift function safe
ELOP Status info_1.ELOP Status_hydraulic sensor fail
ELOP Status info_1.ELOP Status_tilt sensor fail
Secondary pulley revolution sensor failure status
RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: Primary pulley revolution sensor failure status
vehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer order number for a kit (assembled parts bought as a whole for production e.g. cockpit), when the spare part number designates only the server (e.g. for aftersales).

The record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot.Mileage
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000 nor for Clip import

Test.Description: Lost Communication with ECM(C1A-HS FD CAN)

Device.Name: Lost Communication with SCU(5th generation CAN)

Label.Text: Vehicle Identification Number

Screen.Name: Clear QR code and learning data

Label.Text: Clear QR code and Learning data
ATCU is replaced by new one >> Please use manual request function
1. Extract QR code from old ATCU using Service $221600
2. Replace New one, write QR code using service $2E1600+QR code from old ATCU
3. Ignition OFF and wait about 3 seconds
CVT is replaced by new one
1. Prepare QR code(The package of CVT includes CD-ROM)
2. Erase QR code ($31011802) in below condition
R range, Vehicle stopped, Engine stopped, Ignition ON, Accelerator pedal ON
3. Write QR code from CD-ROM using $2E1600+QR code (use manual request)
4. Ignition OFF and wait 3 seconds
5. Replace the CVT, and make ignition ON
If QR code is not in EEPROM, self learning flag is displayed as "self learning NOK"
All calibration data delete status
Self learning data delete status
C/V calibration data delete status
Torque management
Gear shifts management

Display.DataName: Paddle down switch Paddle up switch.Paddle Down status
Display.RequestName: DataRead.Paddle down switch Paddle up switch

Display.DataName: Paddle down switch Paddle up switch.Paddle Up switch
Display.RequestName: DataRead.Paddle down switch Paddle up switch

Label.Text: ATCU inputs/outputs management

Display.RequestName: DataRead.Primary pulley revolution sensor failure status

Label.Text: This screen allow to ignore ABS malfunction due to run on dynamo bench with rear wheels fixed
If the screen isn't displayed in real time, you can use manual reporting request
Don't forget to initialize screen with green button then start reading

Button.Text: To ignore ABS malfunction, to get normal lock-up behavior
To come back to complete diagnostic (normal status)

Label.Text: After G-Sensor replacement, you need to initialize it to "zero" value
Turn ignition switch ON, engine OFF, vehicle at standstill, shift lever in P, brake pedal pressed
CAUTION : Never engine start
CALIBRATE G SENSOR
If G sensor zero calibration status is still "Routine under execution", click until it is changed "Routine completed" or "Routine failed"
EOP Air Bleeding
You need to bleed air for generating proper oil pressure from EOP
it is recommended to execute this routine when vehicle assembly or the replacement of CVT unit so that EOP is good status initially.
CAUTION : Never engine start
Turn ignition switch ON, engine OFF, shift lever in P
